purpose: Record the Unreal preview-map and capture automation run after the first-party procedural material recipe manifest and production geospatial attachment ledger became required inputs, after unreal_log_summary: Using first-party procedural material recipe plan: unreal/Content/RaftSim/Rendering/first_party_procedural_material_recipes.json
Using production geospatial attachment ledger: physics/data/real_world/production_geospatial_attachment_ledger.json
Replaced broad opaque source-drape overlay plates with first-party feathered, terrain-lit microtiles that sample local water and vegetation masks per vertex.
Added first-party near-field water surface grain to the base river ribbon with higher mesh resolution, fine-ripple displacement, dark slicks, and subtle bright streaks.

Added first-party near-camera water macro-ripple mottle to the base river ribbon and river-eye center cover with non-linear shadow/highlight patches and small bounded relief, guarded against straight center seams and still preview-only before production water shader approval.
Added first-party base water depth/current grading to the river ribbon with muted river-specific deep water, center current tongues, sediment threads, and sky-thread variation.

Added first-party base water flow-thread texture directly to the generated river ribbon so broad water planes receive bounded long-current shadow/highlight threads, tiny relief, and flow-scaled foam glints without authorizing solver-driven hydraulics or feature forcing.

Added first-party flow-cued water foam/slick mottle directly to the base river ribbon so flow-band energy seeds bounded aeration flecks and dark current slicks without claiming solver-driven foam or production water shader approval.
Added first-party flow-dependent hydraulic aeration mats, wave-train foam laces, and bounded spray beads over authored flow bands without changing solver authority or hiding conservation failures.

Switched the active first-party vertex-color water preview material back to a bounded DefaultLit normal-response scaffold with low emissive fill, roughness, and specular constants so generated water mesh relief can catch sun/skylight after central water plate and stripe artifacts were demoted.
Muted South Fork and Pacuare foliage base tints, reduced generic proxy emissive lift, and lowered branch/frond leaf-tip highlights for first-party photographic foliage tone calibration.
Corrected the soft-lit terrain material emissive override to a low photographic lift so sun/skylight and mesh normals shape terrain proxies.
Raised the generated terrain luma floor, reduced terrain-feature overlay strength, and softened proxy terrain normals so coarse bank/canyon/rainforest meshes no longer collapse into black plates.
Added bounded terrain ambient fill at 0.16, below the rejected flat 0.42 terrain override, to keep coarse generated slopes readable without claiming final terrain lighting.

Added first-party base terrain material grain and microrelief directly to the generated terrain mesh so broad bank/canyon/rainforest surfaces have source-mask-aware mottle and small normal-scale relief without adding more overlay plates.

Added first-party source-aware terrain photo mottle and microrelief so bank, canyon, and rainforest terrain vertices carry bounded mineral, sand, leaf-litter, wet-toe, and fine surface breakup directly on the base mesh.
Added first-party source-conditioned far-bank albedo and microrelief calibration so broad slopes receive stronger reviewed drape/mask influence while the near-water readability lane stays demoted.

Added first-party broad-slope terrain exposure fill and low-frequency relief so far banks, canyon walls, and rainforest gorge slopes remain readable without turning wet edges or water masks into painted slabs.

Added first-party source-aware macro terrain ridge/facet relief so generated canyon, valley-bank, and rainforest slopes receive bounded mesh relief plus paired shadow/highlight facets without floating overlay plates.

Added first-party source-aware terrain slope facet texture directly to the base generated terrain mesh so broad banks, canyon walls, and rainforest gorge slopes receive bounded sun-face, crease, strata/vegetation color, and relief variation instead of reading as single-color proxy walls.

Added first-party source-aware riparian canopy mass texture directly to the base generated terrain mesh so South Fork and Pacuare banks get denser vegetation-shadow/leaf breakup while Colorado gets muted scrub/sand variation without spawning more icon-like tree props.
Demoted first-party source-overlay plate artifacts by reducing source aerial microtile density/height/weight plus terrain material and Landscape/Nanite scaffold facet counts, sizes, and contrast.
Demoted graphic waterline ribbons by narrowing wet-bank and gravel/mud ribbons, lowering lift and color gain, adding intermittent visibility breakup, and reducing shallow-water edge-band blend.

Added first-party source-masked bank/bar microgeometry so exposed-bar micro pebbles and bank-slope material flakes add small geometric gravel, sand, leaf-litter, and strata breakup without claiming production terrain assets.

Rounded the first-party guide-seat foreground proxy by switching the visible bow, frame bar, tube seams, bow line, and oar shafts to cylinder geometry and shrinking the remaining flat floor/blade pieces.
Demoted terrain overlay striping by reducing terrain material facet, Landscape/Nanite scaffold, strata microband, and slope-occlusion counts, sizes, and color contrast.

Added a first-party source-drape smoothing pass that clamps bright source imagery, reduces aerial microtile density and blend weight, and lowers bank/rill/scaffold overlay density so source-derived terrain cues stop reading as hard painted bands.
Segmented wet-bank and source-detail ribbons by lowering their width, height, color gain, continuity, and secondary band count so waterline review cues do not read as continuous painted slabs.
Demoted near-frame wet-bank, shallow-water, and aeration scaffold lines so foreground review frames preserve the downstream river read.

Demoted pale bank/shoreline scaffold slash artifacts by reducing wet-bank rail, terrain-material facet, and Landscape/Nanite scaffold width, relief, and color influence so source/material cues remain without long bright synthetic slashes dominating river-eye frames.

Demoted pale foam slash artifacts by shrinking foam-ribbon width and blending generated foam/wave cues back toward each river's base water color so bright placeholder marks stop dominating downstream review frames.
Demoted procedural source-detail contour bands to zero active bands in the preview renderer while preserving narrower waterline/gravel ribbons for review cues.
Raised the bounded terrain readability fill from 0.16 to 0.22 after contour demotion, staying below the rejected flat 0.42 override while keeping dark proxy banks reviewable.

Demoted remaining water overlay slab artifacts by pushing water-shader scaffolds, hydraulic mats, glints, current streaks, and foam/ribbon overlays farther downstream while lowering width, opacity, count, and color contrast.

Demoted long dark water streak artifacts baked into the generated river ribbon by disabling dark current-tongue/slick gains for review and reblending base water colors toward the river-specific water tint.

Tightened current-streak, flow-texture, shallow-edge, and waterline-rail artifact demotion by reducing explicit overlay count, length, width, tint strength, and near-frame reach while moving the water shader scaffold farther downstream.

Demoted the remaining central water scaffold plate and base-ribbon dark guide stripe by moving the depth/reflection/refraction scaffold to 200 m downstream, narrowing its width, lowering reflection gain, reducing base river center-current/slick gains, and reblending toward base water color.

Demoted base-water center guide-stripe artifacts with odd cross-river tessellation, center-stripe breakup noise, near-field center-current demotion, stronger centerline reblending, centerline flow-ribbon fading/culling, and broad center-lane erase blending; residual foreground seam risk is now tracked by a dedicated erase pass before production shader/WaterBody replacement.
Set explicit current-streak overlay tint to zero for review so base-water cross-channel breakup carries subtle flow texture without relying on dark guide-line overlays.

Added first-party base-water cross-channel breakup to the generated river ribbon with subtle flow-aware shadow/highlight bands, center-seam diffusion, and tiny bounded relief without changing solver authority.

Added first-party base-water residual center-seam erase by reblending the base ribbon centerline, damping tiny centerline relief, applying a bounded base-water dark-streak luma floor, demoting dark micro-ripple candidates back toward base water color, culling centerline flow texture ribbons, moving center-biased hydraulic mats off the middle of the river, moving the water-shader scaffold to the far review corridor, disabling explicit refraction-seam ribbons, and using odd cross-steps plus scaffold center reblending without changing solver authority.

Added first-party fine twig canopy lace geometry to source-aware and dense biome foliage crowns so tree silhouettes break into smaller twig/leaflet clusters without claiming production foliage assets.

Added first-party foliage crown depth and leaflet breakup so mask-guided darker crown interiors and smaller deterministic leaflets reduce the flat foliage-card read without claiming production foliage assets.

Disabled stylized PCG sample tree and seedling meshes for photoreal review maps so the first-party procedural foliage-only path drives trees, scrub, understory, branch/frond lattice, twig lace, and crown breakup.
Added a first-party procedural canopy height and massing profile so the sample-free foliage path keeps river-specific tree height, crown elevation, and broader organic canopy silhouettes.
Added first-party procedural canopy tone compression and crown-shadow profiling so bright generated greens blend toward darker river-specific canopy anchors.
Demoted first-party foliage card/canopy artifacts by thinning mask-aware canopy cards, reducing rainforest vine curtains, shrinking procedural crown blobs, and lowering leaf-card width/brightness.

Demoted remaining square foliage/source-card artifacts by lowering bank texture card counts, scale, opacity, and silhouette-card visibility while keeping source-aware placement scaffolds for later production foliage replacement.

Culled remaining square card scaffolds by sharply reducing visible bank/source cards, atmospheric/backdrop cards, and blocky foliage proxy cluster counts, scale, and opacity, and replacing canopy/vine/desert scrub upright planes with organic first-party branch/frond geometry where a visible marker is still useful.

Culled blocky bank ecology and instanced sphere foliage proxy artifacts by replacing deadfall/root cube meshes with horizontal cylinder geometry and disabling visible instanced sphere canopy/understory blobs while retaining organic branch/frond/twig foliage passes.
Added first-party source-aware sky gradient and atmosphere-depth sheets so the preview horizon has river-specific sky warmth, haze, and humid depth without claiming final sky/weather assets.

Hardened raft-hidden river-eye evidence capture by hiding foreground raft/oar proxy actors through scene-capture actor/component filtering, primitive-component visibility flags, temporary editor/game actor visibility, and a temporary textured river-eye-only water-color center artifact cover, then restoring or destroying those helpers after capture.

Added a first-party textured river-eye center artifact cover with bounded depth/current mottle and ripple relief for raft-hidden environment-review captures without claiming production water shader approval.

Saved all three generated preview maps with softened source-drape overlays, boulder-contact foam laces, guide-seat foliage setback and scale tuning, source-aware bank breakup texture patches, Saved guide-seat and river-eye captures for South Fork American, Colorado River Grand Canyon, and Pacuare River Rainforest.
Environment preview capture manifest saved successfully.

Created 18 first-party review-only Material Instance Constant assets under unreal/Content/RaftSim/Materials/MaterialInstances from first_party_material_instance_candidates.json; production material promotion remains open.

Assigned review-only MI_RaftSim_*_AtlasCandidate material instances to saved preview-map durable terrain, water, boulder, foliage, and raft proxy surfaces; this exercises the Unreal material handoff but does not promote captures to lifelike evidence.
current_decision: This run validates the rebuilt editor module and refreshed preview assets after the material recipe gate, including creation of nine review-only first-party atlas Texture2D assets, the M_RaftSim_AtlasSampleReview parent material with albedo/normal/AO/roughness/height sampler wiring plus PreviewColor, VertexColorWeight, and RoughnessFloor controls, 18 review-only material instance candidates parented to that graph, and saved preview-map durable surface assignment to those review MICs. The captures remain preview-only and not lifelike until production material promotion, guide/geospatial review, hazard readability, and performance gates pass.
notes: Created 9 first-party review-only Texture2D atlas assets under unreal/Content/RaftSim/Rendering/ProceduralTextureAtlases/Textures and bound them to the 18 MI_RaftSim_*_AtlasCandidate review material instances; production material promotion remains open.

Created M_RaftSim_AtlasSampleReview as a review-only parent material that samples AlbedoAtlas, NormalAtlas, and AORoughnessHeightAtlas through AtlasTileOriginScale, AtlasBlendWeight, NormalIntensity, RoughnessScale, and HeightScale; production material promotion remains open.

Assigned the review-only material instances to durable terrain, water, boulder, foliage, and raft proxy surfaces in the saved preview maps; this is material handoff evidence, not lifelike art approval.

Post-capture quality review records all six current captures as preview-only with low-detail/proxy blockers; this blocks lifelike screenshot approval until visual replacement work clears the metrics and human review.
